bootstrap(uint256,uint256).details: Factory must approve this contract for (2 * amount) USDH         before calling. Recommended amount: 50–200 USDH per side.

params.isYes: true = buy YES, false = buy NO

claim().details: Three scenarios handled:  A) Normal market — both sides had traders     → Winners split all collateral pro-rata. Standard winner-takes-all.  B) One-sided market — winning side had traders, losing side had NONE     → Winners are fully refunded their collateral (minus fees).       There was no real counterparty, so no winnings to claim — but       crucially, nobody loses money to an empty market.  C) INVALID market     → Everyone gets their collateral back pro-rata, regardless of side.

placeLimitOrder(bool,uint256,uint256).details: Orders sit in the book. When a market buy comes in that         would cross the spread, it fills resting orders first         (at their limit price) before hitting the AMM.         This gives limit order makers a price improvement.

params.isYes: true = buy YES, false = buy NO
params.priceLimit: max price willing to pay (PRICE_SCALE units)                     e.g. 550000 = willing to pay up to 55¢ for YES
params.usdhAmount: USDH to commit (locked until filled or cancelled)

reclaimBootstrap().details: Safe to call any time after resolution — bootstrap USDH         is tracked separately and never mixed with trader funds.

bootstrap(uint256,uint256).notice: Called by the factory immediately after deploy.         Seeds both sides of the AMM with real USDH so that         every market has a liquid counterparty from block 1.

buy(bool,uint256,uint256).notice: Buy YES or NO shares — fills limit orders first, then AMM

cancelOrder(uint256).notice: Cancel an unfilled limit order and get USDH back

getOrderBook(uint256).notice: Get open limit orders near the current price (for UI order book display)

getQuote(bool,uint256).notice: Quote a trade — reads order book + AMM to give accurate estimate

placeLimitOrder(bool,uint256,uint256).notice: Post a limit order — "I want to buy YES at ≤ X cents"

reclaimBootstrap().notice: Factory calls this after resolution to reclaim its bootstrap         liquidity. Must be called after all traders have claimed.

resolveInvalid().notice: Mark INVALID — triggers full pro-rata refund path

resolveManual(bool).notice: Resolve via trusted resolver (sports, earnings, macro)

resolveWithOracle().notice: Resolve via dual-oracle confirmation.         Calls aggregator.confirmPrice() which:           1. Reads HyperCore primary price           2. Reads secondary price (posted by off-chain monitor)           3. Reverts if either is stale or they deviate > 1%         Only settles using the confirmed average. If sources         disagree, resolution is blocked until they converge.

sell(bool,uint256,uint256).notice: Sell shares back before market closes
